endpoints.name: Get namespaces and repos
endpoints.description: Gets a list of your namespaces and repos which have data available
endpoints.notes: Replace {{DOCKER_API_KEY}} with your DOCKER API KEY.

endpoints.name: Get namespace
endpoints.description: Gets metadata associated with specified namespace, including extra repos associated with the namespace
endpoints.notes: Replace {{DOCKER_API_KEY}} with your DOCKER API KEY. Replace {{NAMESPACE}} with a real namespace.

endpoints.name: Get years with data
endpoints.description: Gets a list of years that have data for the given namespace
endpoints.notes: Replace {{DOCKER_API_KEY}} with your DOCKER API KEY. Replace {{NAMESPACE}} with a real namespace.

endpoints.name: Get timespans with data
endpoints.description: Gets a list of timespans of the given type that have data for the given namespace and year
endpoints.notes: Replace {{DOCKER_API_KEY}} with your DOCKER API KEY. Replace {{NAMESPACE}} with a real namespace. Replace {{TIMESPANTYPE}} with a real timespantype. Replace {{YEAR}} with a real year.

endpoints.name: Get namespace metadata for timespan
endpoints.description: Gets info about data for the given namespace and timespan
endpoints.notes: Replace {{DOCKER_API_KEY}} with your DOCKER API KEY. Replace {{NAMESPACE}} with a real namespace. Replace {{TIMESPANTYPE}} with a real timespantype. Replace {{TIMESPAN}} with a real timespan. Replace {{YEAR}} with a real year.

endpoints.name: Get namespace data for timespan
endpoints.description: Gets a list of URLs that can be used to download the pull data for the given namespace and timespan
endpoints.notes: Replace {{DOCKER_API_KEY}} with your DOCKER API KEY. Replace {{TIMESPANTYPE}} with a real timespantype. Replace {{DATAVIEW}} with a real dataview. Replace {{NAMESPACE}} with a real namespace. Replace {{TIMESPAN}} with a real timespan. Replace {{YEAR}} with a real year.

endpoints.name: Create an authentication token
endpoints.description:
Creates and returns a bearer token in JWT format that you can use to
authenticate with Docker Hub APIs.

The returned token is used in the HTTP Authorization header like `Authorization: Bearer {TOKEN}`.

Most Docker Hub APIs require this token either to consume or to get detailed information. For example, to list images in a private repository.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{DOCKER_API_KEY}} with your DOCKER API KEY.
